meta.name: Italy
meta.aboutStudying: Italy is home to the world's oldest university (Bologna, 1088) and some of Europe's most beautiful cities. Public universities charge low tuition (€1,000–4,000/year), and the combination of world-class education, extraordinary culture and Italian lifestyle makes it deeply attractive.

tips.title: Visa & Permits
tips.text: EU citizens need only their ID. Non-EU students need a student visa and permesso di soggiorno (residence permit). Apply through your local Italian consulate. The Universitaly pre-enrollment portal must be used for non-EU applicants.

tips.title: Cost of Living
tips.text: Italy varies enormously. Milan costs €1,100–1,700/month; smaller cities like Bologna or Turin cost €700–1,100. Italian university canteens (mense) serve excellent food for €3–6. DSU scholarships cover fees and housing for lower-income students.

tips.title: Language
tips.text: Most bachelor's programmes are in Italian. Italy has one of the fastest-growing selections of English-taught master's programmes in Europe — Politecnico di Milano and Bocconi are leaders. Italian language courses are widely available and highly recommended.

universities.name: Sapienza University of Rome
universities.desc: Europe's largest university by enrolment — Sapienza sits at the heart of Rome with faculties spanning every discipline and an extraordinary history of scholarship.

universities.name: University of Bologna
universities.desc: The world's oldest continuously operating university, founded in 1088, with some of Europe's best law, medicine and engineering programmes.

universities.name: Politecnico di Milano
universities.desc: Italy's #1 technical university — world-ranked for engineering and design, with a location in Europe's fashion and business capital.

universities.name: Università degli Studi di Milano
universities.desc: Milan's largest comprehensive public university, offering a huge range of programmes in one of Europe's most dynamic economic capitals.

universities.name: Bocconi University
universities.desc: Europe's top business and economics school — consistently ranked #1 in Italy and top 10 in Europe for business and management.

universities.name: University of Florence
universities.desc: Set in the world's greatest Renaissance city, the University of Florence combines exceptional arts and humanities with strong medical and scientific research.

universities.name: University of Padua
universities.desc: Italy's second-oldest university, near Venice, renowned for its medical school (Galileo and Harvey studied here) and strong sciences and engineering.

universities.name: Politecnico di Torino
universities.desc: Italy's second-best technical university after Politecnico di Milano — particularly strong in automotive engineering, given its location near Fiat/Ferrari headquarters.

universities.name: University of Turin
universities.desc: One of Italy's top comprehensive universities with exceptional strength in law, economics and medicine in Italy's most industrial northern city.

universities.name: University of Naples Federico II
universities.desc: Southern Italy's largest and most prestigious university, founded by Holy Roman Emperor Frederick II — strong in engineering, law and medicine.

universities.name: Università Ca' Foscari Venezia
universities.desc: The university of Venice — located in spectacular palazzo buildings throughout the historic city, with strong international business, languages and environmental studies.

universities.name: LUISS Guido Carli University
universities.desc: Italy's leading private university for law, economics and political science — the preferred feeder school into Italy's political, business and media elite.

universities.name: University of Pisa
universities.desc: One of Italy's most respected universities — home of the Scuola Normale Superiore campus. Strong in engineering, physics and CS in Galileo's city.

universities.name: University of Milano-Bicocca
universities.desc: Milan's modern north-campus university — strong in medicine, statistics and social sciences, with one of Italy's best clinical medicine schools.

universities.name: Università Cattolica del Sacro Cuore
universities.desc: Italy's largest private university — prestigious Catholic institution with campuses in Milan, Rome and Brescia, particularly strong in medicine and law.

universities.name: University of Bari Aldo Moro
universities.desc: Southern Italy's leading comprehensive university after Naples — strong in medicine, law and agriculture in the vibrant Adriatic city of Bari.

universities.name: University of Palermo
universities.desc: Sicily's largest university in its magnificent Baroque capital — offering the full range of disciplines with strong presence in medicine and engineering.

universities.name: University of Catania
universities.desc: Sicily's second-oldest university in a city of Baroque architecture and Mount Etna — strong in physics, engineering and agricultural sciences.

universities.name: University of Genoa
universities.desc: Historic maritime university in one of Italy's most atmospheric port cities — strong in naval and industrial engineering, medicine and law.

universities.name: University of Pavia
universities.desc: Italy's fourth-oldest university, 30 minutes from Milan — famed for producing Nobel laureates in physics (Volta) and medicine.

universities.name: University of Siena
universities.desc: One of Italy's oldest universities in the most beautiful medieval town in Tuscany — offering medicine, law and economics in a living museum of Gothic art.

universities.name: University of Salerno
universities.desc: Southern Italy's fastest-growing technical university near the spectacular Amalfi coast.

universities.name: University of L'Aquila
universities.desc: A modern Abruzzi mountain university rebuilt after the 2009 earthquake — very affordable fees with a strong engineering and physics department.

universities.name: University of Perugia
universities.desc: In Umbria's spectacular hilltop capital — ancient university (1308) particularly strong in agriculture, medicine, law and the arts.

universities.name: University of Ferrara
universities.desc: A historic university in a UNESCO World Heritage Renaissance city — strong combination of medicine, engineering and architecture.

universities.name: University of Modena and Reggio Emilia
universities.desc: Italy's oldest Studium in the centre of the Motor Valley — adjacent to Ferrari, Lamborghini and Maserati, with excellent automotive and mechanical engineering.

universities.name: University of Verona
universities.desc: Young but rapidly rising university in the city of Romeo and Juliet — strongest in medicine and economics with a growing international English-taught offer.

universities.name: University of Brescia
universities.desc: Focused technical-medical university in Lombardy's second city — strong in medicine, engineering and economics with excellent industry links to Brescia's manufacturing sector.

universities.name: Università della Calabria
universities.desc: Southern Italy's most innovative campus university — a self-contained city-campus known for strong engineering and computer science departments.

universities.name: University of Rome Tor Vergata
universities.desc: Rome's technical and scientific university — particularly strong in engineering, physics and medicine.

universities.name: Roma Tre University
universities.desc: Rome's youngest public university — innovative and internationally minded, particularly strong in architecture, engineering, law and political science.

universities.name: University of Trento
universities.desc: Consistently ranked in Italy's top 5 — Trento punches above its size with excellence in law, sociology and computer science in the spectacular Dolomite foothills.

universities.name: Scuola Normale Superiore
universities.desc: Italy's most elite postgraduate institution — free for all admitted students. Produces extraordinary researchers across sciences and humanities.

universities.name: Politecnico di Bari
universities.desc: Southern Italy's leading technical university — engineering, architecture and design programmes at the gateway to the Adriatic and the Balkans.

universities.name: Università Politecnica delle Marche
universities.desc: The Marche region's polytechnic — particularly strong in mechanical engineering, fisheries, marine science and medicine on Italy's Adriatic coast.

universities.name: University of Udine
universities.desc: A well-resourced modern university in Friuli, at the crossroads of Italian, Slavic and Austrian cultures, with strengths in engineering and agriculture.

universities.name: University of Trieste
universities.desc: In Italy's most Central European city on the Adriatic. Strong in physics (world-leading ICTP research institute is here), engineering and law.

universities.name: University of Cagliari
universities.desc: Sardinia's main university in a strikingly beautiful capital — offering the full spectrum of programmes on an island renowned for biodiversity and a unique ancient culture.

universities.name: University of Sassari
universities.desc: Sardinia's second university — historic institution with very low fees and particular strengths in agriculture, veterinary science and medicine.

universities.name: University of Salento
universities.desc: In the baroque city of Lecce — the 'Florence of the South'. Strong in cultural studies, engineering and sciences in one of Italy's most charming and affordable cities.

universities.name: IULM University
universities.desc: Milan's creative and communication university — highly regarded for advertising, public relations, tourism management and foreign languages in Europe's media capital.

universities.name: Università IUAV di Venezia
universities.desc: The world's most uniquely located architecture school — studying the built environment while living in a floating Gothic-Byzantine-Renaissance masterpiece city.

universities.name: Free University of Bozen-Bolzano
universities.desc: Italy's only trilingual university — all degrees taught in Italian, German and English. Set in the Alps at the crossroads of Latin, Germanic and Rhaeto-Romanic cultures.

universities.name: University of Campania Luigi Vanvitelli
universities.desc: Named after the architect of the Royal Palace of Caserta — campuses across Campania with notable strengths in medicine, architecture and engineering.

universities.name: University of Insubria
universities.desc: A young university between Como and Varese on the Swiss border — strong in natural sciences, medicine and economics, offering an Italo-Swiss cross-border experience.

universities.name: Humanitas University
universities.desc: Italy's only fully English-taught medical school — the 6-year medicine degree entirely in English, linked to Humanitas Research Hospital, one of Italy's top clinical research centres.

universities.name: University of Messina
universities.desc: On the Strait of Messina between Sicily and mainland Italy — historic university with a strong medical school and pharmacy faculty.

universities.name: University of Macerata
universities.desc: One of Italy's oldest universities in a beautiful Marche hilltop town — specialising in law, humanities and political science at very accessible fees.

universities.name: University of Basilicata
universities.desc: The university of Italy's least-explored region — very low fees, strong in agricultural sciences, engineering and environmental studies near the UNESCO Sassi di Matera landscape.

universities.name: Università per Stranieri di Siena
universities.desc: Italy's oldest university for foreigners — the world's leading institution for Italian language, culture and civilisation teaching in the most beautiful town in Tuscany.

universities.name: Università per Stranieri di Perugia
universities.desc: Italy's second foreigners' university drawing students from over 160 countries — a world leader in Italian language teaching in Umbria's ancient hilltop capital.

universities.name: SISSA – International School for Advanced Studies
universities.desc: Italy's most prestigious graduate research institution — fully English-taught and state-funded. World-renowned for theoretical physics, mathematics and neuroscience.

universities.name: University of Eastern Piedmont
universities.desc: A young accessible university across Vercelli, Novara, Alessandria and Asti — offering affordable degrees in medicine, pharmacy, law and business in northern Italy's rice and wine country.

universities.name: University of Chieti-Pescara
universities.desc: G. d'Annunzio University on the Adriatic coast of Abruzzo — strong in medicine, dentistry and architecture with a scenic coastal location and very low student costs.

universities.name: LIUC – Cattaneo University
universities.desc: A small focused private university near Milan — offering engineering, business and law with excellent industry connections and very high graduate employment rates.

universities.name: LUMSA – Libera Università Maria SS. Assunta
universities.desc: A Catholic private university in central Rome with campuses in Palermo and Taranto — focused on law, communication, education and political science.

universities.name: Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome
universities.desc: Rome's leading private medical research university — linked to a 300-bed teaching hospital, offering cutting-edge medicine and biomedical engineering.

universities.name: Università degli Studi di Napoli Parthenope
universities.desc: Naples' maritime and economic university overlooking the Bay of Naples — specialising in nautical and aeronautical engineering, economics and law with a dramatic seafront campus.

universities.name: University of Parma
universities.desc: In the food capital of the world — set in the city of Parmigiano-Reggiano, prosciutto and Verdi's music. Strong in food science, medicine and pharmacy.

universities.name: Università Mediterranea di Reggio Calabria
universities.desc: At the very tip of the Italian boot overlooking Sicily — specialising in architecture, civil engineering and agronomy in Italy's most southerly big city.

universities.name: University of Molise
universities.desc: The smallest public university in mainland Italy in the rural Molise region — very affordable degrees in agriculture, biotechnology, law and environmental sciences.

universities.name: LIUC – Carlo Cattaneo University
universities.desc: A compact private university near Varese — known for industrial engineering, logistics and management with strong ties to Lombardy's manufacturing sector.

universities.name: University of Messina
universities.desc: Sicily's oldest university on the Strait of Messina — strong in medicine, pharmacology and engineering with some of Italy's lowest tuition fees.

universities.name: University of Enna 'Kore'
universities.desc: Sicily's private university in the island's mountainous interior — accessible entry, affordable fees and a growing reputation for digital arts and engineering.

universities.name: University of L'Aquila
universities.desc: Rebuilt after the 2009 earthquake — a resilient mountain university known for earthquake engineering, geophysics and mathematics in the Gran Sasso national park.

universities.name: University of Teramo
universities.desc: An Abruzzo university at the foot of the Gran Sasso — particularly strong in veterinary medicine and food science near Italy's unspoilt Adriatic coast.

universities.name: Niccolò Cusano University
universities.desc: A modern private university offering both on-campus and distance-learning degrees across Italy — accessible entry with competitive fees across law, engineering and health sciences.

universities.name: Guglielmo Marconi University
universities.desc: Named after the inventor of radio — a fully e-learning private university with flexible degree programmes ideal for working students.

universities.name: Free University of Bozen-Bolzano
universities.desc: Europe's only trilingual public university — operating in Italian, German and English in the heart of the Dolomites, at the crossroads of Italian and Alpine culture.

universities.name: University of Urbino Carlo Bo
universities.desc: In the perfect Renaissance hill-town of Urbino — particularly strong in biomolecular science, pharmacy and law in a UNESCO city built for scholarly beauty.

universities.name: University of Cassino
universities.desc: Near the WWII battlefield of Monte Cassino — a young practical university particularly strong in automotive engineering, with research links to the nearby Fiat-Stellantis plant.

universities.name: University Suor Orsola Benincasa
universities.desc: Naples's historic private university — strong in law, literary studies and teacher training in one of the most character-rich cities in the Mediterranean.

universities.name: International Telematic University UNINETTUNO
universities.desc: The world's most multilingual e-learning university — delivering degrees in 5 languages with collaborations from the Arab League to the European Space Agency.

universities.name: NABA – Nuova Accademia di Belle Arti
universities.desc: Italy's leading private art and design academy — alumni work for Prada, Versace and Ferrari; top-ranked for fashion design, graphic design and media arts.

universities.name: Domus Academy
universities.desc: The world's first postgraduate design school — founded in Milan's fashion district, alumni design products for Apple, Ferrari and the world's most prestigious fashion houses.

universities.name: Politecnico di Torino
universities.desc: Turin's technical university and Fiat's founding institution — particularly strong in automotive engineering, aerospace, ICT and sustainable energy in Italy's motor capital.

universities.name: University of Eastern Piedmont Avogadro
universities.desc: A compact multi-campus public university in Piedmont — offering medicine, pharmacy and economics at very affordable rates across Vercelli, Novara and Alessandria.

universities.name: University of Catanzaro Magna Græcia
universities.desc: Named after the ancient Greek civilisation of southern Italy — dedicated primarily to health sciences, medicine and pharmacology in Calabria.

universities.name: Kore University of Enna – extended
universities.desc: Sicily's private university focused on digital transformation and economic development in the island's interior — affordable and growing rapidly.

universities.name: University of Camerino
universities.desc: One of Italy's oldest universities in a perfectly preserved Renaissance hill-town — specialist in pharmacy, veterinary science and environmental geology.

universities.name: University of Foggia
universities.desc: Apulia's agriculture university in the Tavoliere plain — strong in agri-food technology, veterinary medicine and economics in one of Italy's sunniest regions.

universities.name: University of Sannio
universities.desc: In the ancient Samnite capital of Benevento — strong in renewable energy engineering, CS and economics in an affordable Campania setting.

universities.name: Politecnico di Bari
universities.desc: Southern Italy's leading technical university — strong in civil, mechanical and environmental engineering, serving the booming Apulia tech and renewable energy sector.

universities.name: University of Salerno
universities.desc: Campania's second university — one of the largest in southern Italy, with strong engineering, informatics and economics on a large campus south of Naples.

universities.name: Catholic University of the Sacred Heart – Brescia
universities.desc: The Brescia campus of Italy's largest private university — strong in medicine, economics and legal studies with deep ties to the Lombardy business community.

universities.name: SDA Bocconi School of Management
universities.desc: Bocconi's world-ranked business school — top 5 in Europe for MBA, SDA alumni run UniCredit, Pirelli and half of Italy's FTSE MIB boardrooms.

universities.name: American University of Rome
universities.desc: An American liberal arts university in the heart of Rome — offering US-accredited bachelor's degrees entirely in English, an ideal choice for American students studying abroad.

universities.name: University of Bologna – Business School
universities.desc: Bologna's executive education powerhouse — offering MBA and master's programmes through Italy's oldest university, with deep industry ties across Emilia-Romagna.

universities.name: ESCP Business School – Turin Campus
universities.desc: The Turin outpost of Europe's oldest business school — offering European master's degrees at the gateway to France and the Alps.

universities.name: Venice International University
universities.desc: A unique inter-university institution on the island of San Servolo in the Venice Lagoon — offering intensive international degree programmes co-taught by Ca' Foscari and leading world universities.

universities.name: John Cabot University
universities.desc: A US-accredited liberal arts college in Rome's Trastevere neighbourhood — one of the most popular destinations for American study-abroad students in Europe.

Rome.desc: Rome is eternal — 2,800 years of continuous history layered on top of each other in the most extraordinary city on earth. As a student, you walk past the Colosseum, the Pantheon and the Spanish Steps on your way to lectures. Sapienza, the world's largest university, is at the heart of it all.
highlights: Colosseum & Forum — the beating heart of the ancient Roman world
Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel — Michelangelo's masterpiece
Trastevere — the most charming student neighbourhood, full of trattorias
Borghese Gallery — Italy's finest private art collection
Piazza Navona — Baroque masterpiece of fountains and street life
Rome.studentLife: 200,000 students in Rome across multiple universities. Trastevere, Pigneto and San Lorenzo are the student districts. Accommodation is €450–700/month for a shared room. Sapienza's campus is enormous.
Rome.transport: Metro (2 main lines + 1 new), buses, trams. Monthly pass ~€35. Rome is best navigated by bike or scooter. Trenitalia high-speed to Florence in 1h30.
Rome.nightlife: Trastevere for classic Roman bar crawls, Pigneto for alternative bars, Testaccio for clubs in old slaughterhouse buildings. Romans eat late (9pm) and stay out until 4 AM.
pros: Walking past the Colosseum every day — there is no comparison
Most affordable capital city in Western Europe for students
Extraordinary food at every price point — pizza al taglio from €1.50
World's highest concentration of museums, churches and archaeological sites

Milan.desc: Milan is Italy's engine — the financial, fashion and design capital that drives the country's economy. Politecnico di Milano and Bocconi are genuinely world-class. The pace is faster, the ambitions are higher, and the career opportunities are incomparable compared to any other Italian city.
highlights: Duomo Cathedral — Italy's most impressive Gothic cathedral with free rooftop access
Brera district — the artists' neighbourhood full of galleries and aperitivo bars
Navigli district — canal-side bars, vintage markets and aperitivo culture
La Scala — one of the world's greatest opera houses
Fondazione Prada — contemporary art in a stunning Rem Koolhaas-designed complex
Milan.studentLife: Huge international student community at Politecnico and Bocconi. Navigli, Brera and Isola are the key student areas. More expensive than other Italian cities but salaries post-graduation are proportionally higher.
Milan.transport: 5 Metro lines (expanding), trams, buses, BikeMi bike-share. Monthly pass ~€39. Malpensa Airport 45 minutes by train to central station.
Milan.nightlife: Navigli for aperitivo culture and canal-side bars, Colonne di San Lorenzo square for outdoor student gatherings, Lambrate for alternative clubs and art spaces.
pros: Italy's best job market — fashion, finance, design, consulting all headquartered here
Bocconi & Politecnico ranked among Europe's top institutions
Milan Fashion Week, Salone del Mobile, major international events
Fast train to Venice (2h), Florence (1h45) and Rome (3h)

Florence.desc: Florence is quite simply the world's greatest open-air museum. Michelangelo, Leonardo da Vinci, Dante and Botticelli all worked here — and the city still looks much as they would have known it. The University of Florence is a comprehensive institution embedded in one of the most beautiful cities ever built.
highlights: Uffizi Gallery — Botticelli's Venus, Raphael, Leonardo, all under one roof
Duomo & Brunelleschi's Dome — the engineering marvel of the Renaissance
Ponte Vecchio — the medieval bridge lined with goldsmiths
Oltrarno district — the genuine Florentine neighbourhood across the river
Piazzale Michelangelo — panoramic sunset view over the terracotta rooftops
Florence.studentLife: 60,000 students, many of them international (it's the most popular city in Italy for American study-abroad programmes). The Santa Croce and Santo Spirito areas are student-heavy. Rent: €500–700/month for a shared room.
Florence.transport: Buses (ATAF/Linea) and extensive cycling. Florence is compact — the historic centre is entirely walkable. Train to Rome in 1h30, to Milan in 1h45 (Frecciarossa).
Florence.nightlife: Oltrarno for aperitivo and wine bars (enotece), Santa Croce for student bars, Fiesole hills for summer outdoor events. Florence is more sophisticated than party-heavy.
pros: The world's finest collection of Renaissance art as your daily backdrop
Compact and completely walkable historic centre
Florence study abroad — the most international student community in Italy
Exceptional Italian language immersion — Florentine is considered 'pure' Italian

Bologna.desc: Bologna gave the world the university — founded in 1088, the world's first. Called La Dotta (The Learned), La Rossa (The Red) and La Grassa (The Fat) for its scholarship, leftist politics and extraordinary food. It's the most student-dense city in Italy and has one of the richest food cultures on earth.
highlights: Two Towers (Asinelli & Garisenda) — medieval towers that define the skyline
Piazza Maggiore — Bologna's vast and beautiful civic heart
Mercato di Mezzo — ancient covered market, recently restored and buzzing
Basilica di San Petronio — one of Italy's largest Gothic churches
Archiginnasio — the original 16th century university building with anatomical theatre
Bologna.studentLife: Bologna is 25% students — it's the most student-saturated city in Italy. Everything is geared towards student life: the cheapest Emilian food, aperitivo culture, left-wing politics. The university is literally all around the city.
Bologna.transport: Bus network across the city, Tper services to surrounding towns. Flat and compact — a cycling paradise. High-speed train to Milan in 1 hour, Rome in 2 hours.
Bologna.nightlife: Via del Pratello for alternative bars, Piazza Verdi for student outdoor gatherings (and free open-air cinema in summer), Zona Universitaria for all-night bars.
pros: World's first university — the original student city
Best food in Italy (Emilia-Romagna is the undisputed culinary capital)
25% student population means the whole city is a campus
Most politically engaged and culturally active city in Italy

Turin.desc: Turin is Italy's most underrated city — the former royal capital of the House of Savoy, birthplace of Italian unification, and home to the country's automotive industry (Fiat, Ferrari, Lamborghini all started here). Politecnico di Torino is one of Europe's leading technical universities and the city is reinventing itself as an innovation hub.
highlights: Mole Antonelliana & National Cinema Museum — Turin's iconic tower and the world's best film museum
Egyptian Museum — the most important Egyptian collection outside Cairo
Piazza Castello — the magnificent Baroque heart of the royal city
Reggia di Venaria Reale — Italy's Versailles: 80,000 sq m of royal palace
Porta Palazzo market — Italy's largest open-air market
Turin.studentLife: 100,000 students across Politecnico and the University of Turin. San Salvario is the student district. Turin is significantly more affordable than Milan while offering exceptional technical education.
Turin.transport: Metro (1 line), trams, buses and a bike-sharing scheme. Very flat — easy to cycle everywhere. TGV to Paris in 5 hours, Milan in 45 minutes by train.
Turin.nightlife: San Salvario for student bars and multicultural restaurants, Murazzi riverfront for clubs, Quadrilatero Romano for aperitivo culture.
pros: Politecnico di Torino — Italy's best engineering university outside Milan
Most affordable major northern Italian city
Automotive engineering capital — Ferrari, Fiat, Lamborghini careers
Alps and skiing 1 hour from the city centre

Naples.desc: Naples is the loudest, most chaotic, most passionate city in Italy — the birthplace of pizza, mozzarella and a philosophy of life that prioritises beauty, family and pleasure over efficiency. Federico II University is one of Italy's most prestigious, and the city's food, history and raw energy are unlike anything else in Europe.
highlights: Pompeii & Herculaneum — the Roman cities frozen by Vesuvius in 79 AD (30 min by train)
National Archaeological Museum — the world's greatest collection of Greco-Roman art
Spaccanapoli — the street that literally splits the ancient city in two
Castel dell'Ovo — Norman seafront castle with harbour views
Sfogliatella and pizza — the best food experiences of your life, for €2
Naples.studentLife: 130,000 students in Naples. The university is embedded throughout the historic centre. Accommodation is very affordable: €250–400/month. The city rewards students who engage with it.
Naples.transport: Metropolitana (3 lines), funiculars up the hills, Circumvesuviana trains to Pompeii. The historic centre is best explored on foot. Ferries to Capri, Ischia and Amalfi Coast.
Naples.nightlife: Spaccanapoli for evening passeggiata, Chiaia neighbourhood for upmarket aperitivo, Vesuvio seafront for summer clubs. Naples at night feels like a film set.
pros: Cheapest major city on this entire list
The world's greatest pizza — for €1.50 at Da Michele
Pompeii, Capri and the Amalfi Coast right on your doorstep
Most intensely alive city in Italy

Venice.desc: Venice is the world's most unique city — 118 islands connected by 400 bridges, no cars, and canals instead of streets. Ca' Foscari University puts students right in the historic centre. It's expensive, but the experience of living and studying in Venice is genuinely unlike anywhere else on earth.
highlights: Piazza San Marco — the heart of Venice
Grand Canal — the main waterway through the city
Rialto Bridge and Market — 16th century icon, still a market
Biennale Pavilions — world's most important art event
Vaporetto water bus network — public transport by boat
Venice.studentLife: Ca' Foscari and IUAV architecture school put over 20,000 students in the historic centre. Living in Venice is expensive but extraordinary. Dorsoduro is the student neighbourhood. Vaporetto monthly pass is a student staple.
Venice.transport: Vaporetto (water bus) — the entire city is navigated by boat or on foot. Airport is 15 minutes by boat. Mestre mainland (5min by train) has cheaper accommodation and normal transport.
Venice.nightlife: Campo Santa Margherita in Dorsoduro is the student piazza — bars spill onto the square every evening. Spritz is the ritual. Clubs are few; this is a city for aperitivo culture.
pros: Most unique city in the world to study in
Ca' Foscari ranked among Italy's best for languages and economics
Architecture and arts environment is unparalleled
World Biennale Art and Architecture events at your doorstep

cityCards.desc: The eternal city — Colosseum, Vatican, 2,800 years of history and Europe's largest university (Sapienza).
Italy's financial and fashion capital — home to Bocconi, Politecnico and the best career opportunities in the country.
The cradle of the Renaissance — Uffizi, Michelangelo's David, the Duomo, and the world's finest art in one walkable city.
The world's first university city (1088) — the most student-dense city in Italy with the best food on earth.
Italy's royal capital reinvented — Politecnico di Torino, automotive industry (Fiat, Ferrari) and the Alps 1 hour away.
The loudest, most passionate city in Italy — birthplace of pizza, Pompeii 30 min away and the cheapest major city on the list.
